your_book:
Monday brought a split session for Jaleel's book — tech momentum carried the top two positions while defensive healthcare dragged. ADBE surged 3.12% (now worth $3,613) and NOW added another 3.3% ($2,559), both riding what looks like a broader AI-infrastructure bid. The two together represent 28.5% of the book, so that tailwind mattered. Against them, Abbott slid 1.94% ($2,671) despite being the second-largest position and carrying a STRONG BUY and an 81.8 QARP score — with the next earnings print just three days out (Jul 16) and an EPS guide already trimmed, the price action deserves attention rather than dismissal. RKLB was the session's ugliest number: down 5.32% to $1,995, now sitting at −17.6% in Jaleel's book and carrying an AVOID verdict with a 40.7 QARP — every down day widens the gap between where the price is and where any thesis would need to be. NVDA also fell 3.52% ($1,328), retreating despite still being up 10.9% in the book; the Capitol Hill quantum/export-control noise is a real overhang.

Drift worth naming: BSX is flagged for a guidance slash — FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5%, management guiding flat through Q3 — yet still holds a STRONG BUY verdict and an 82.3 QARP. At $1,116 and −6.7% in the book, the model conviction and the operational reality are pulling in different directions. LULU moved up modestly (+0.87%) but five straight quarters of negative Americas comps and a guidance cut keep the thesis under pressure; next read isn't until Sep 2. The overall book closed with an unrealized loss of −$62.95 (−0.29%) on $21,627 in total value, with $75 cash on the sidelines.

What to watch: ABT earnings on Jul 16 is the nearest binary event. NOW reports Jul 22 — as the single largest weight in the book at 11.8%, any guide-down on that call would hit harder than the ticker weight alone suggests. NVDA's export-control story and the Senate scrutiny around AI capex aren't resolved; the Aug 25 earnings print is the formal checkpoint but the headline risk is live now.

attention.headline: AVOID-rated position down 17.6% and falling further
attention.note: RKLB dropped another 5.32% today, now −17.6% in the book, and the system's own verdict is AVOID with a 40.7 QARP — the data offers no basis for continuing to hold, and each session without a decision compounds the loss.

attention.headline: Earnings in 3 days with guide already cut
attention.note: Abbott reports July 16 with EPS guidance already trimmed; at 12.4% of the book it's a large position and any further cut or revenue miss could move the needle materially — Jaleel needs a pre-earnings stance now, not after the print.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed yet conviction rating unchanged — thesis drift
attention.note: FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3, but BSX still carries STRONG BUY and 82.3 QARP; the gap between model conviction and stated guidance needs a reconciliation before the Jul 29 print.

attention.headline: Largest position reports in 9 days at a high multiple
attention.note: At 11.8% weight and up 16.8% in the book, NOW has the most to give back if Jul 22 guidance disappoints — any hawkish-Fed or IT-budget commentary could reprice the whole position fast.
